high.rationale: Git metadata is traversal noise and already hard-excluded by EXCLUDED_FOR_CODE_GRAPH.
Installed dependency trees are reproducible package-manager output and already excluded by scanner defaults plus root .gitignore.
false_positive_examples: Package-manager fixture tests may intentionally model node_modules trees; use explicit fixture include overrides instead of indexing all installed deps.

high.rationale: Python bytecode cache; source authority is the .py file.
Pytest runtime cache; explicitly ignored by pytest and Python ignore templates.
Local Python environment/dependency install, analogous to node_modules.
Runtime compile cache excluded as temporary output.
Local semantic-index database/cache, not source for code-graph symbols.

medium.rationale: Usually compiled/package output, but distribution fixtures and checked-in browser/package bundles can be meaningful regression context.
false_positive_examples: Open-source package/test fixtures sometimes keep built distribution trees; dist means distribution, not always disposable source.

medium.rationale: Often build output, but segment-style matching can hit legitimate source package names.
false_positive_examples: bazelbuild/bazel: src/main/java/com/google/devtools/build contains canonical Java source.

medium.rationale: Often output, but expected-output fixtures and APIs with 'out' naming can be test-critical.
false_positive_examples: pytorch/pytorch: committed test_out_dtype_op.py is source-like regression coverage around out APIs.

medium.rationale: Vendored dependencies are often bloat, but may be required for dependency graph fidelity.
false_positive_examples: kubernetes/kubernetes: vendor/ contains committed Go module source and modules.txt.

medium.rationale: Local repo treats external as temporary/non-canonical, but other monorepos use external integrations as source inputs.
false_positive_examples: Bazel/TensorFlow-style ecosystems may expose external repositories or wrappers under external-like paths.

medium.rationale: Generated code is often derivable noise but can be the checked-in public API/client surface.
false_positive_examples: Generated API/client repositories can commit generated sources as the implementation users consume.

low.rationale: Fixtures are often intentionally committed and semantically useful; iteration 4 warned not to globally exclude fixtures except under known excluded roots.
false_positive_examples: eslint/eslint: tests/fixtures is committed test source/context.
golang/go: src/cmd/go/testdata contains committed Go testdata files.

low.rationale: Scratch can mean throwaway work, imported sample repos, or active investigation material depending on repo policy.
false_positive_examples: This repo has scratch paths under spec archives/templates that may be useful for migration or historical debugging.

low.rationale: Repo lifecycle convention, not ecosystem-wide generated/noise signal.
false_positive_examples: This repo's z_archive contains prior spec packets and scratch repos that can be important historical context.

low.rationale: Repo lifecycle convention for future/experimental work; can contain real source-like prototypes and research packets.
false_positive_examples: This repo's z_future contains agentic-system and hybrid-RAG research packets.

low.rationale: Repo-specific ignored product spec trees; useful when the operator targets those product areas.
Root .gitignore marks this as not part of the framework, but it is a local project-area decision.
false_positive_examples: This repo's working tree includes /a_nobel_en_zn as a real project directory for non-framework tasks.

low.rationale: Root .gitignore marks this as not part of the framework, but it is a local project-area decision.
false_positive_examples: This repo's working tree includes /ai_systems as a real project directory for non-framework tasks.
